name: Front Squat
resistance_profile: Upright torso shifts emphasis to the quads through a deep range.
execution_steps: Set the bar in the rack and hold it across the front of your shoulders with elbows high (cross-arm or clean grip).
Unrack, step back, and set feet shoulder-width with toes slightly out.
Brace hard and sit straight down, keeping your torso upright and elbows lifted.
Descend to at least parallel, then drive up through your whole foot to stand.
Re-brace at the top and repeat, then rack after your last rep.
good_when: You want a squat that biases the quads with a more upright torso.
You want to reduce lower-back shear versus the back squat while still loading heavy.
You have the wrist and shoulder mobility to hold a solid front rack.
bad_when: You're a beginner still learning to squat (the front rack adds complexity).
Wrist or shoulder mobility prevents a stable rack position.
You want the absolute most axial load — the back squat allows more.
